headline: EXPERTS CALL $7,000 GOLD — Silver Hits Decade Highs Up 150% YoY, US-Iran Ceasefire Fuels Third Weekly Win
snippet: Gold surged over 4% in the week on US-Iran ceasefire news, a weaker dollar, and growing rate-cut expectations. Silver climbed to $75.6/oz — up 150% year-over-year to decade highs — with some analysts now modelling $7,000/oz gold under a sustained central bank and safe-haven buying scenario.

headline: Gold ALREADY Beat Its Full-Year 2026 Bank Forecasts After Q1 — Targets Being Reset to $4,500–$5,000+
snippet: Gold averaged a record $4,873/oz in Q1 2026, already exceeding nearly every major bank's full-year target set in January. GoldSilver.com revisits the scoreboard and finds structural drivers — central bank demand, dollar weakness, and rate-cut expectations — still firmly in place heading into Q2.

headline: JPMorgan: Gold Miner PULLBACK Is 'Compelling Buying Opportunity' — Record Cash Flows Still Not In Multiples
snippet: JPMorgan analysts argue the recent ~30% decline in gold mining equities — while the metal itself held near record highs — has created a compelling entry point. The bank notes miners are generating record cash flows and margins that are still not reflected in their compressed equity multiples.

headline: RESERVE DEPLETION Crisis: Gold and Silver Miners Can't Replace What They Mine — Why It's Long-Term Bullish
snippet: Sprott Money's 2026 outlook highlights declining reserve replacement ratios across major gold and silver producers. As exploration budgets fail to keep pace with depletion and new large-scale discoveries become harder to find, the structural floor on future production keeps falling — a paradoxically bullish setup for metal prices.

headline: EXTREME Gold-Silver Ratio Points to Major Readjustment — Historically, One Metal Must Move Sharply
snippet: With the gold-to-silver ratio at historically extreme levels, GoldSeek analysis argues a significant repricing is overdue. Past extremes have resolved with sharp moves in one or both metals — and with silver already up 150% year-over-year, the ratio is beginning to compress in silver's favour.

headline: Copper: Structural DEFICIT Meets Tariff Chaos — Volatile 2026 Ahead Even With Strong Long-Term Fundamentals
snippet: Mining.com maps the competing forces in copper for 2026: a structural supply deficit beginning to emerge as demand from data centres, EVs, and grid expansion accelerates — versus US tariff risk and China demand uncertainty that could inject extreme near-term volatility into what should be a straightforward bull case.

headline: JPMorgan Keeps Copper As TOP PICK — $12,500/t Q2 Target, AI Data Centres to Absorb 475,000t Alone
snippet: J.P. Morgan's copper market outlook maintains a constructive view, projecting $12,500/tonne by Q2 2026 as AI data centre construction alone is forecast to absorb 475,000 metric tons of copper this year. The bank sees a structural deficit emerging as supply growth fails to match accelerating demand.

headline: AI Boom Set To TURBOCHARGE Uranium Demand — Nuclear Now the Only Baseload That Scales With Data Centres
snippet: Surging AI infrastructure investment is creating electricity demand that renewables alone can't satisfy, reinforcing the case for nuclear power and further tightening the uranium supply outlook. Mining.com reports that AI data centre load growth has vaulted to become one of the top-tier drivers of utility uranium contracting.

headline: SPROTT: Uranium Bull Market Intact — Tightening Supply, Rising Reactor Demand, And Gov't Policy Support
snippet: Sprott's 2026 uranium outlook finds the structural bull case intact despite Q1 volatility: reactor capacity additions are outpacing new mine supply, the US Section 232 framework has formally designated uranium as a national security priority, and institutional involvement in uranium equities is growing rapidly.

headline: BATTERY METALS Spotlight: Structural Deficits Looming For Most Critical Minerals As Demand Outpaces Supply
snippet: The Northern Miner's global battery metals spotlight finds structural demand deficits projected to grow over the next decade for most critical minerals. Companies are scrambling to find and build new supply — but the gap between current exploration investment and the volumes required to meet future demand has rarely looked wider.

headline: Mining M&A Deals To WATCH in 2026 — Bigger Companies, Bigger Targets, Biggest Price Tags Since 2012
snippet: Mining Indaba surveys the most significant M&A transactions unfolding in 2026 as the sector's consolidation cycle enters its most aggressive phase in over a decade. International capital — including moves by Chinese and Middle Eastern players into North American and African assets — is now a dominant deal driver.

headline: JUST HOW MUCH GOLD Is There? — Scarcity, Not Just Demand, Is The Hidden Driver Of The Bull Market
snippet: With only an estimated 54,770–64,000 tonnes of economically mineable gold remaining globally, Money Metals Exchange argues the bullish case for gold isn't just about demand — it's about the fundamental scarcity of a finite resource that is increasingly expensive to find and extract at current prices.
